articles.title: Man charged in Lowell hit-and-run that killed woman, 29
articles.description:
Guilherme Candido, 23, is charged with leaving the scene of personal injury and death following a fatal crash, records show.

The post Man charged in Lowell hit-and-run that killed woman, 29 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Guilherme Candido, 23, charged in Lowell hit-and-run that killed 29-year-old woman.
Candido faces charges for leaving the scene of the fatal crash.
The incident raises concerns about road safety in Lowell and surrounding Boston areas.

articles.title: Mass. detective, in kayak, allegedly shot hole in canoe with his brother on it
articles.description:
James Miller, 52, allegedly fired at least one round of gunfire into the bottom of his brother's canoe, police said.

The post Mass. detective, in kayak, allegedly shot hole in canoe with his brother on it appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Detective James Miller, 52, allegedly shot his brother's canoe while kayaking.
The incident involved at least one round of gunfire into the canoe.
Local authorities are investigating the incident for potential criminal charges.

articles.title: MLB owners propose a salary cap for the first time since baseball’s 1994-95 strike
articles.description:
MLB's proposal would cap spending in 2027 at $245.3 million, using figures for luxury tax payrolls that include $20.1 million for benefits and the pre-arbitration bonus pool.

The post MLB owners propose a salary cap for the first time since baseball’s 1994-95 strike appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: MLB owners propose a salary cap of $245.3 million starting in 2027.
Proposal includes $20.1 million for benefits and pre-arbitration bonuses.
Boston teams may adjust spending strategies impacting local player acquisitions.

articles.title: 5 takeaways as Payton Tolle and Chris Sale battle before Braves pull away for 10-2 victory
articles.description:
The Red Sox are now 9-19 at home and haven’t won a series at Fenway Park since early April.

The post 5 takeaways as Payton Tolle and Chris Sale battle before Braves pull away for 10-2 victory appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Red Sox lose 10-2 to Braves, continuing poor home record.
Team now 9-19 at Fenway Park this season.
Last series win at home was in early April.

articles.title: Drake Maye reflects on Super Bowl loss, rewatching game film vs. Seattle
articles.description:
"For me, on the biggest stage and a big game, and I had a lot of plays I wish I had back."

The post Drake Maye reflects on Super Bowl loss, rewatching game film vs. Seattle appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Drake Maye reflects on Super Bowl LX loss to Seattle Seahawks.
Maye expressed regret over key plays during the game.
Boston residents follow Maye's journey as he aims for improvement.

articles.title: Former Bruins enforcer, radio host Lyndon Byers diagnosed with CTE after his death
articles.description:
"I will do whatever I can to make sure nobody else has to watch their loved one deteriorate like that.”

The post Former Bruins enforcer, radio host Lyndon Byers diagnosed with CTE after his death appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Lyndon Byers, former Bruins enforcer, diagnosed with CTE posthumously after his death.
Byers advocated for awareness of brain injuries in sports, impacting local youth hockey programs.
His diagnosis raises concerns about player safety and mental health in Boston's sports community.

articles.title: Claude Lemieux, the feisty 4-time Stanley Cup champion for Avalanche, Devils and Habs, dies at 60
articles.description:
As a player, the 6-foot-1, 215-pound Lemieux was a bruising mix of talent and abrasiveness, not afraid to cross the line in the name of competition over 21 seasons in the NHL.

The post Claude Lemieux, the feisty 4-time Stanley Cup champion for Avalanche, Devils and Habs, dies at 60 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Claude Lemieux, a 4-time Stanley Cup champion, died at age 60.
Lemieux played 21 NHL seasons, known for his aggressive playing style.
His legacy influences local youth hockey programs in Boston.
